name: The Fool
upright:
Gain proficiency in one random skill, tool, language, weapon, and armour type. Roll or draw to determine each.
Learn three random cantrips from any spell list.
Gain a feat of your choice, even if you don't meet its prerequisites (it must align with one of the random proficiencies or spells obtained through this card).
You are immediately teleported to a random location (DM's choice)—chosen for its narrative potential, danger, or strange opportunity.
You leave behind all current possessions, but gain:
A Clothing set appropriate for your skill and language proficiency.
A random tool matching your new tool proficiency.
One magic weapon and one magic piece of armour that correspond to your newly gained proficiencies.
Two random Wondrous magic items.
Your alignment shifts to a randomly determined one that is not your current alignment. (Curable)
You gain a permanent flaw: "You must accept at least one risky or irrational opportunity presented each day."
For the next 7 days:
You roll with advantage on all dice rolls.
Random minor beneficial miracles and coincidence happen, as if the world bends slightly in your favour.
reversed:
The creature becomes paralysed with existential fear for 24 hours (Curable). If the creature is immune to being frightened, it loses its immunity. Afterward, they permanently lose one random skill proficiency, known language, and attuned item.
For the next 7 days:
You cannot benefit from advantage. All dice rolls are made normally, even when advantage would apply.
You have disadvantage on Insight, Perception, and Initiative checks, as your senses dull in self-isolation. (Curable)
You are unable to attune to new items, form new bonds (e.g. familiar, steed, pact), or gain inspiration during this time.
Any attempt to begin a new journey, quest, or relationship is magically sabotaged (DM's narrative control; subtle misfortunes).

name: The Magician
upright:
You gain expertise in one skill you're already proficient in.
You may choose a spell that requires concentration to master, whilst concentrating on this spell you may concentrate on another at the same time.
You immediately choose three 9th-level spells which you can cast at any point during the next year consuming the spell charge in the process.
You gain the permanent flaw: "You feel compelled to demonstrate your power publicly."
For the next 7 days, any spell you cast counts as if cast with a spell slot one level higher, without extra slot cost (up to a maximum of 9th). If you are unable to cast spells, then this ability can be gifted to any other creature you think of at this moment. The creature under this effect receives a massive pointy wizard hat a colour and design of your choosing folded out of the card and can concentrate on two spells at once.
reversed:
Permanently lose one random skill expertise or proficiency if you have no expertise.
Your cantrips and spells known is both reduced by 1. (Curable)
You are targeted and followed by an Antimagic Field for 7 days the area effected is increased to 20 feet.

name: The High Priestess
upright:
Your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 2 (to a maximum of 22).
You may ask the DM one question about the future (at any point within the next year). The DM must answer truthfully, though vaguely if appropriate.
For the next 7 days:
You can cast either True Seeing, Legend Lore, or Commune once a day, at will.
You have advantage on Insight checks, and you always know when someone is lying.
reversed:
You become blinded and deafened for 24 hours as the silence of the divine overwhelms you (Curable). If the creature is immune to being blinded or deafened, it loses its immunities.
For the next 30 days:
You have disadvantage on Insight, Arcana, Religion, and Perception checks. (Curable)
You are incapable of benefiting from divination magic (e.g. Guidance, Augury, Divination, etc. fail or fizzle).
You suffer both a short, long, and indefinite madness.
Dreams become disturbing, paranoid, or incomprehensible, giving you no rest. If you fail a DC 15 Wisdom save at the start of each long rest, you gain one level of exhaustion (which does not go away unless the save succeeds). (Curable)

name: The Empress
upright:
Within the next 7 days choose an object, plant, or creature you currently carry or touch. It becomes permanently enhanced:
Objects become magical, gain an additional enchantment, and become sentient (perceiving you as an ally).
Plants bloom endlessly, resisting withering or decay, and become sentient (perceiving you as an ally).
Creatures gain +2 to one ability score (max 22) and one permanent beneficial mutation (extra sense, faster speed, etc. DM's discretion).
You gain the permanent flaw: "You become overly protective of something trivial."
For the next 7 days, your hit points maximum increases by 20, and any source of healing targeting you is maximised.
reversed:
You become cursed with Sterile Touch for 30 days: any plant, food, or natural material you touch begins to wilt, rot, or decay.
You gain a permanent flaw: "I cannot stand to see others flourish without my involvement."
For the next 7 days:
You have disadvantage on any skill check made to work with a tool.
You cannot regain hit points through magical healing, and potions rapidly spoil in your possession. (Curable)
If you currently have a familiar, pet, steed, or bonded creature, they become hostile or flee until the effect ends. This also affects other's familiars.

name: The Emperor
upright:
Your Charisma score increases by 2 (to a maximum of 22).
A deed appears across the card, naming you the rightful ruler of a distant, formidable fortress currently overrun by enemies. You must reclaim it to make your dominion real. If reclaimed the card folds into a golden crown.
When wearing the crown, you may issue a spoken command or speech to all creatures who can hear and understand you. They must follow your order to the best of their ability until it is fulfilled, unless doing so would directly end their life or violate their fundamental nature. You may only issue one order that must be able to be completed within 7 days, when fulfilled the crown withers away.
For the next 7 days, you cannot be moved against your will (immune to forced movement, teleportation, charm, and fear).
reversed:
Your Charisma score is reduced by 2.
If you lead a group, army, or faction, your command crumbles, NPC allies become disloyal, confused, or hostile. No orders are followed without conflict.
You gain a permanent flaw: "I believe I must control everything, even those closest to me."
For the next 7 days:
You have disadvantage on all Persuasion and Intimidation checks. (Curable)
No creature willingly follows your orders, even magically compelled ones (e.g. Command, Dominate Person) — they resist or misinterpret them.
Any settlement or organization that currently favours you becomes suspicious or hostile, reacting as if you've betrayed them.

name: The Hierophant
upright:
For the next 30 days, a Deva, Planetar, or Solar becomes your devoted companion. If you are evil aligned, this may be a fallen version with distorted purpose (DM's choice).
You gain proficiency in Religion (or expertise if already proficient), and can read, write, and speak Celestial or Infernal (depending on your alignment).
You receive the Blessing of the Sacred Order — a boon from a divine entity. Choose one:
Immunity to being Charmed
Resistance to Radiant and Necrotic damage
+1 permanent increase to your Wisdom score
Your appearance shifts slightly towards that of an angel or devil depending on alignment.
Clerics, paladins, and devout NPCs treat you as a sacred authority for 1 year, unless you openly defy their god or doctrine.
reversed:
Your Wisdom score is reduced by 1.
A celestial or divine avatar immediately appears in front of you sent to judge you, appearing in a form tied to your world's pantheon. It may test, punish, or forgive.
You gain a permanent flaw: "I no longer trust institutions or those who preach."
Holy symbols within 30 ft. flicker, tarnish, or crack while you are nearby. (Curable)
You are excommunicated by a major religious force or divine entity.
For the next 7 days you cannot benefit from healing spells or divine blessings (including Lay on Hands, Bless, Divine Intervention).
Religious orders become hostile toward you for the next year. Zealots, inquisitors, or clerics may actively hunt you as a heretic.

name: The Lovers
upright:
The drawer and the nearest humanoid creature within 60 feet are struck by an overwhelming magical love, as if they have shared a lifetime of passion and connection.
If no creature is present, the effect targets the first humanoid the drawer sees within 60 feet afterwards (DM's discretion).
A pair of resplendent engraved platinum rings (worth 5,000 gp each) appear on each creature's appendage. They are considered magically bonded for 7 days. The rings cannot be removed by any means during this time; if an appendage is severed, the ring reappears elsewhere on the creature's body. After 7 days, the rings can be removed normally. (Curable)

While bonded, the following effects apply:
The two cannot willingly move out of eyesight of each other.
While within 60 feet of each other, they are both affected by Warding Bond, targeting each other, and have a +2 bonus to AC. (Damage redirected by Warding Bond does not recursively trigger additional damage between the pair.)
The two share half of all healing they receive (rounded up).
If one drops to 0 hit points, the other (if not incapacitated) immediately must expend half of their maximum hit dice healing the other and taking psychic damage equal to the rolled amount.
If one dies, the other must succeed on a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw or die instantly of heartbreak.
reversed:
The drawer becomes magically obsessed with the creature they hate the most (DM's discretion).
For the next 7 days, the following effects apply:
The drawer cannot attack, harm, or speak ill of that creature. (Curable)
The drawer will comply with that creature's wishes, even if threatened or endangered. (Curable)
The drawer will do everything in their power to protect the creature, becoming hostile to anything perceived as a threat. (Curable)
If near the creature, the drawer actively attempts to gain its affection, behaving irrationally or manipulatively to do so. (Curable)
If the chosen creature dies during this time, the drawer immediately suffers 12d12 psychic damage and gains 5 levels of exhaustion.

name: The Chariot
upright:
Your movement speed increases by 10 feet, and you ignore the effect of difficult terrain.
You can Dash or Disengage as a bonus action.
You gain the ability to cast steel wind strike once per day without expending a spell slot.
You gain proficiency in all vehicles. Beasts and monstrosities with an Intelligence of 4 or lower automatically regard you as a trusted rider.
Travel becomes easier within the region for 30 days: roads clear, weather favours journeys.
A war-chariot of spectral design, pulled by two elemental beasts (DM's choice — fire, ice, shadow, light, etc.), materialises and is bound to you for 7 days. While mounted in the chariot:
You gain advantage on saving throws against being grappled, pushed, restrained, or frightened.
The chariot can traverse any terrain—including water, vertical surfaces, or thin air — as though under the effects of spider climb and water walk.
reversed:
Your movement speed is permanently reduced by 5 feet.
If you had any vehicle proficiencies, you immediately lose them. If you had none, you instead lose proficiency in a random skill.
You gain a permanent flaw: "I must always keep moving, no matter the consequences."
For the next 7 days:
Whenever you attempt to move more than 15 feet on your turn, roll a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you move in a random direction instead (roll 1d8). If the direction is blocked or hazardous, you fall prone. (Curable)
You cannot benefit from Dash or Disengage actions. (Curable)
Mounts and vehicles you attempt to use actively resist you — bucking, stalling, or even attacking you.

name: Strength
upright:
Your Strength score becomes 22. If your Strength is already equal to or greater than 21, it is increased by 2 instead.
You gain proficiency in Animal Handling (or expertise if already proficient), and beasts are non-hostile toward you unless provoked.
Choose one creature type (e.g., Beast, Giant, Dragon, Undead). You gain a passive ability thematically tied to that type (DM's choice, such as darkvision, damage resistance, or a climbing speed).
reversed:
Your Strength score is set to 1. Each day thereafter, it increases by 1 until it reaches 2 below what it was before drawing the card. It then remains there. (Curable; each successful attempt increases the score by 1.)
For the next 7 days, you have disadvantage on all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution checks and saving throws. (Curable)
For the next 30 days, beasts and animals treat you as hostile, regardless of your actions.

name: The Hermit
upright:
You gain proficiency in Insight (or expertise if already proficient).

You gain the ability to summon a spherical demiplane shaped by your subconscious, as the card drops into your hand depicting an illustration of your imaginative plane.

Time is suspended outside the sphere while it passes normally within. The demiplane's duration depends on how many creatures you bring with you (all of whom are placed back where they were after it ends):
- 1 (just yourself): 1 year
- Up to 4: 1 month
- Up to 12: 1 week
- Up to 50: 1 day
- Up to 200: 8 hours
- Up to 1,000: 1 hour
- Up to 10,000: 10 minutes
- Over 10,000: 1 minute

You may mentally choose who to bring, and the space sizes itself appropriately.
You may cast spells and rest as normal inside. No one can be harmed or killed within the demiplane.
You have advantage on all checks related to determining your own condition, desires, or memories.
The effect can be ended early at will, expelling all inhabitants harmlessly.
Once used, this ability cannot be used again by any means besides drawing this card again.
reversed:
You vanish, your body and mind suspended in a state of stasis within an extradimensional sphere, utterly severed from the material world, functioning as an ethereal prison of your own mind.

All equipment and items you carry disappear with you, except for artifacts, which fall to the ground in your place.
You cannot be located by any form of divination magic. Only a wish spell can reveal the location of your prison.
You are conscious but unable to escape through your own means. You do not age, require sustenance, or dream.
You remain imprisoned indefinitely until someone finds and releases you.
You draw no further cards.

name: Wheel of Fortune
upright:
You may choose to draw any number of additional tarot cards. For each card drawn this way, it is drawn face up, and you can choose the order of the draw.
If you chose one or more cards, you draw an additional one. You then may force one of these draws upon another creature in sight.
For the next 7 days you may reroll natural 1's.
reversed:
You must immediately draw 1d4 + 1 additional tarot cards. Any harmful effects from these cards are doubled (at the DM's discretion).
For the next 7 days, any natural 20's you roll are rerolled. (Curable)

name: Justice
upright:
The card folds itself into a glowing flaming halo hovering above your head, as you take a vow to righteousness, gaining the following benefits:
Your Wisdom score increases by 2 (to a maximum of 24).
You gain proficiency in Insight.
You gain truesight out to 120 feet.
Creatures within 120 feet of you cannot lie by magical, verbal, or written means. Attempts to deceive or obscure the truth automatically fail, and others instinctively know when a speaker is being evasive.
You are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions.
Your weapon attacks and spells deal an additional 1d8 radiant damage.
Religious orders treat your word as law and abide by it.
If you break your vow by lying, committing a crime, or taking an unjust action (DM's discretion), you immediately lose all benefits granted by this card.
reversed:
A false crime is magically etched into reality, rewriting the memories and records of one major civilisation. You are now wanted dead or alive for a heinous offence (e.g. murder, treason, heresy — DM's choice).
If you have any past crimes (just or unjust), they are publicly revealed for the world to see from the worst possible angle.
For the next 7 days, any Insight, Zone of Truth, or truesight effect used against you reveals your worst thoughts or most damning memories, true or not. (Curable)

name: The Hanged Man
upright:
You enter a trance-like state for 10 minutes, during which you are incapacitated and unaware of your surroundings. You float slightly above the ground in a meditative posture and cannot be harmed, moved, or manipulated in any way during this time.
When the trance ends, you must permanently sacrifice one ability, possession, or magical boon (DM's discretion), which is removed from your character sheet.
In return, you gain another ability, possession, or magical boon of similar or greater value, shaped by fate or your subconscious desires (DM's discretion).
reversed:
You are immediately paralysed, suspended upside-down in mid-air by invisible forces for 24 hours (Curable). You cannot act or speak during this time. If you are normally immune to being paralysed, you permanently lose that immunity.
You cannot be reduced below 1 hit point while suspended.
For the next 7 days, the following effects apply:
You cannot take reactions. (Curable)
You roll initiative at disadvantage. (Curable)
You have disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws made to escape restraints, grapples, bonds, or imprisonment. (Curable)
You cannot voluntarily end concentration on spells. If you begin concentrating, it continues until disrupted or the spell ends naturally. (Curable)
Your centre of gravity is reversed, causing you to fall upwards instead of down. This applies to all forms of falling or forced movement.
Fall damage is doubled.

name: Death - Version 1
upright:
You die instantly. No resurrection, revival, reincarnation, or wish can return you to life.
Your soul is unmade. It cannot be found or communicated with by any means, including divination, speak with dead, true resurrection, or wish.
Any creature that knew you must succeed on a DC 25 Intelligence (History) check to recall their memories of you. On a failure, they believe you never existed. (Curable)
Your body crumbles into dust, and all non-Artifact items you carry are scattered randomly across the planes.
This effect bypasses immunity to death effects, undeath, or soul-binding protections.
You draw no more cards.

This is considered a "True Death." Only divine intervention from a power beyond the planes may undo this effect—at the DM's narrative discretion.
reversed:
You may immediately resurrect one creature who has died, regardless of how or when. Their soul is remade and returns willingly, even if it was unwilling, lost, or destroyed. No material components are required.
If no creature is chosen, the card folds into a single-use true resurrection spell scroll that you may cast at any time, even if you are dead. When used, your spirit manifests briefly and enacts the spell.
For the next 7 days:
Once per long rest, if you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you instead drop to 1 hit point.
You cannot die by any means during this time.

name: Death - Version 2
upright:
You die instantly as your body, suspended in animation, is encased in a blazing white-golden cocoon, reliving every possible death over the span of 24 hours. Nothing can break this cocoon or remove you from within, and anyone who touches its surface immediately takes 18d10 radiant damage.
After this period, you are immediately reborn as the cocoon breaks apart. You retain all memories and aspects of your personality, but your physical form has been remade.
You must remake your character using a different race and class than before. You retain your same stat array, though you may rearrange your ability scores and skill proficiencies.
You gain an additional +2 skill points to allocate as you wish, and one additional skill proficiency.
All conditions, curses, diseases, and magical effects affecting you end (except this card's permanent changes).
reversed:
You are cursed with the mark of death:
You no longer age. (Curable)
Your creature type changes to Undead, changing both appearance and granting features at the DM's discretion. (Curable)
You have disadvantage on all death saving throws, and cannot be resurrected in any way. (Curable)
You gain a permanent flaw: "I refuse to change. I will cling to what I know, even as it rots around me."
For the next 7 days:
You cannot benefit from long rests. (Curable)
Any attempt to change your form (polymorph, wild shape, reincarnate, wish, etc.) automatically fails. (Curable)
Failed death saving throws are only reset after a long rest. (Curable)

name: Temperance
upright:
Your two lowest ability scores both increase by 2.
You gain proficiency in one saving throw you are not already proficient in, determined by rolling a d6.
For the next 7 days:
Roll two d6s, each representing an ability score. Whenever you make a check, saving throw, or attack roll using one of them, you may use the other instead.
If you are a spellcaster, all your spell slots are temporarily stored in a single shared pool. You can use this pool to cast any spell you know or have prepared, expending a number of slots equal to the spell's level from the pool.
During this period, your concentration on spells cannot be interrupted by anyone but yourself (being incapacitated still breaks it).
reversed:
Your lowest ability score is reduced by 2, and your highest ability score increases by 2.
You gain resistance and vulnerability to one random damage type.
You automatically fail concentration checks. (Curable)
Any prominent character features that are physical or mental are enhanced, whilst lesser traits are hidden away. (Curable)
You gain a permanent flaw: "I act impulsively, even when I know I shouldn't."
For the next 7 days:
Your highest and lowest ability scores are swapped. (Curable)
Any checks, saving throws, and attack rolls not using your highest or lowest ability score are made with disadvantage. (Curable)

name: The Devil
upright:
A powerful fiend takes notice of you. It manifests as a shadowy projection before you and offers a contract — you may refuse or accept.
If you refuse: The fiend becomes your sworn enemy. It begins hunting you, seeking to make your life miserable and, in time, claim your soul through death.
If you accept: You gain the benefits of the fiend's contract (DM's discretion) and the following effects:
Fiends and corrupt entities regard you as kin and will not attack you unless provoked.
You emit a faint scent of brimstone, and your eyes darken with a shadowy glow, as your features and traits transition toward that of a devil.
reversed:
You are instantly freed from all forms of magical control or domination, including curses, geas spells, oaths, or pacts that bind your will. (Curable) effects are also automatically ended. The entity that bound you is aware of this — and may seek vengeance.
For the next 7 days:
You cannot be charmed, frightened, or possessed.
Any deal or agreement you make is automatically tilted in your favour, with the other party remaining oblivious to it.
You automatically succeed on saving throws against temptation or coercion, mundane or magical.
You have advantage on saving throws against any spell cast by fiends, fey, or undead creatures.

name: The Tower
upright:
The card splits into two: one descending into the ground, shattering it in its wake; one ascending into the sky, forming formidable storm clouds — both worsening in intensity over time. After 10 minutes, a crack of thunder echoes as divine lightning descends from the storm, with the ground tearing violently as earthquakes erupt in a 2d4 + 2 mile radius centred on the card draw, triggering the following effects in the area:

The entire area is simultaneously targeted by the effects of earthquake and storm of vengeance for 1 minute, after which a meteor swarm detonates at its centre (all spells DC 21).
All structures, magical barriers, and unattended objects within this area are instantly destroyed or reduced to rubble.
All ongoing magical effects within the area end immediately, as if affected by dispel magic cast at 9th level.
The reign of destruction lessens the further away from the epicentre it is (at the DM's discretion).
The drawer loses all non-magical possessions, and a random magic item in their possession is destroyed.
For the next 30 days:
You automatically fail all saving throws against being knocked prone or restrained. (Curable)
Whenever you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you are struck by residual lightning, taking 4d10 lightning damage and are knocked prone. (Curable)
All structures you sleep or rest in suffer minor, ominous damage by dawn.

Region Environmental Effects:
For the next 30 days, anything built or created has a +10 to its DC. On a failure, all materials used in the process are somehow wasted. On a success, the result contains visible cracks, warped geometry, unsettling asymmetry, or similar flaws — generally feeling wrong.
The next harvest is completely ruined as everything is spoiled.
Localised or natural disasters are far more prone to occur.
reversed:
You are subjected to an effect similar to power word pain combined with regeneration as the tarot card brands itself onto your forehead. (Curable) These effects last for 24 hours, and any creature who touches you comes under the same effects.
A creature who endures these 24 hours to completion is immediately restored to full hit points and ends all conditions, diseases, and (Curable) effects affecting them.
When the effect ends, the card falls into your hands. If it ever touches the ground, it trembles — and from the debris of your past failures, a grandiose fortress rises around it.
For the next 7 days:
You have advantage on saving throws against being knocked prone, stunned, or frightened.
Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you instead drop to 1 hit point and emit a shockwave. All creatures within 30 feet must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw or be pushed 30 feet away, knocked prone, and take 4d10 force damage.

name: The Star
upright:
You are granted the ability to cast the wish spell once, at will and without any drawback.
You gain the feature Fate Weaver. Roll a d20 repeatedly until you roll either a 1 or a 20. You may, at any time, replace a d20 roll with this stored result, expending the feature in the process. While expended, you may roll a new d20 at the start of each day; on a result of 1 or 20, the feature recharges with that corresponding value.
For the next 7 days:
You and all creatures within 30 feet of you are under the effects of beacon of hope.
You emit 30 feet of dim coloured light of your choosing from your skin.
reversed:
Your presence is no longer acknowledged by the fabric of fate.
A beneficial episode or action you took in your life is undone as if you were never there at that critical moment.
You cannot benefit from inspiration, luck-based features, or rerolls of any kind (including Lucky, Portent, Bardic Inspiration, guidance, bless, etc.). (Curable)
Creatures cannot choose you as the target of beneficial magic unless there is no other possible target. Healing, buffs, support spells, Bardic Inspiration — all instinctively "skip" you unless forced, as if you don't exist. (Curable)
You cannot be the subject of prophecy, divination, or destiny-altering magic. All divination spells treat you as if you are "not part of the pattern."
Your maximum hit points are reduced equal to one of your Hit Dice.
For the next 7 days:
Your connection to destiny is severed. Whenever you roll a d20, treat natural 20s as natural 1s.
The skies above you dim noticeably. Any area you rest in becomes unnaturally dark and melancholy.
Creatures struggle to remember small personal details about you. They know you, but the sense of who you are becomes harder to grasp.

name: The Moon
upright:
Your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 2 (to a maximum of 24).
You gain the ability to Dreamstride once per day. The duration depends on the time spent channelling the feature:
At will: 1 round
As a reaction: 1 minute
As an action: 10 minutes
1 minute of focus: 1 hour
10 minutes of focus: 8 hours

While Dreamstriding, you enter a dreamlike trance. Your body becomes incorporeal as per the etherealness spell, though your form remains semi-transparent and silent. You have advantage on Stealth checks during this state, and your speed is equal to 3 × your Wisdom or Intelligence score, whichever is higher.
Your reflection and shadow occasionally lag half a second behind you.
For the next 30 days:
You have advantage on Insight, Perception, and Investigation checks made in moonlight.
All illusions or sensory trickery are revealed to you in their true form.
reversed:
Your Wisdom score is reduced by 1.
You perceive and treat illusions as if they are real. Any illusion you encounter functions fully for you, allowing physical interaction as though it were an actual creature or object.
Your subconscious fractures, manifesting your darker reflection as a physical being:
A hostile Shadow Duplicate of you appears within 120 feet.
It uses your stat block, class features, spells, and equipment.
It attempts to kill you using methods that reflect your own typical tactics.
It is an illusion given substance; other creatures perceive it as illusory.
When destroyed, it dissolves into smoke.
For the next 30 days:
You suffer a short-term, long-term, and indefinite madness simultaneously.
You have disadvantage on Insight, Investigation, and Perception checks. (Curable)
Your sleep is plagued by nightmares. At the start of each long rest, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain one level of exhaustion. This exhaustion does not fade unless you succeed on this check. (Curable)

name: The Sun
upright:
Light follows your path. A pure ray of blinding sunlight descends from the aether, centred on the card. All creatures that can see the card must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute.
The card then transforms into a Bead of Pure Sunlight, a magical item that can be attached to or detached from any other item. While attached, the item becomes magical, requires attunement, and gains the following benefits:
It gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls and spell attack rolls made using it. If the item already had a bonus, it is increased by that amount instead.
It deals an additional 1d8 radiant damage on a hit.
You gain the ability to cast the following spells through the weapon; it has 3 charges, which are restored at dawn:
Daylight (at will). Creatures within this light have advantage on saving throws against being frightened, and advantage on checks and saving throws made to discern illusions.
Wall of Light (1 charge), Sunbeam (2 charges), and Sunburst (3 charges).
For the next 7 days:
You are immune to radiant damage.
You cannot be blinded.
You are immune to any light-based negative effects.
After the 7 days have passed, these benefits are reduced to:
Resistance to radiant damage.
Advantage on saving throws against light-based effects.
reversed:
You become vulnerable to radiant damage.
You immediately lose all special senses (such as darkvision, blindsight, tremorsense, truesight, etc.) except for normal sight.
You then become blinded until the next dusk, as supernatural darkness gathers around you. This darkness:
Extends only 5 feet out from you, but grows in size until it is a 300-foot radius centred on you at the time of dusk.
Cannot be removed, dispelled, illuminated, or seen through by any means.
At the first dusk, the darkness erupts outward and disperses into the sky. From that moment onward, the sun does not rise for the next 2d4 + 4 months.
During this time:
Creatures of the night are significantly empowered.
Nocturnal and light-averse creatures become far more aggressive.
Civilisation falters as fear, scarcity, and unrest spread.
Fires burn but give less comfort.
Food tastes blander.
Laughter feels strained and hollow.

name: Judgement
upright:
The card spins before you, its faces showing past, present, and future all at once. Reality's fabric unravels and rewrites itself at your command.
At any point after drawing this card, you may invoke its power to alter three past events, choosing one of the following keywords. Each keyword may only be used once:
Alter — You change the defining aspect upon which the chosen event hinged. The event still occurs, but its meaning, outcome, or catalyst is different.
Avoid — A specific creature, faction, or nation does not participate in the chosen event. All consequences of their absence ripple forward naturally.
Erase — The chosen event never occurred. All memories, records, and consequences tied directly to it are rewritten or removed.
Condemn/Redeem — The event still occurs, but a creature or faction involved is judged differently. A celebrated victory is revealed as atrocity. A villain's act becomes a sacrifice.
Delay — The event still happens — but years, decades, or centuries later.
Reveal — Nothing changes about the event — but everyone learns the truth.
Testify — The drawer and those within 10 feet become part of the event retroactively. You were there. You made a choice. History now includes you.
The chosen event must be one that the drawer was aware of, witnessed, or was meaningfully affected by. Once invoked, the change is absolute and irreversible, rewriting history, fate, and causality itself.
reversed:
Your presence in the world weakens. All your ability scores are reduced by 1.
All written records, magical archives, and living memories of you vanish:
No creature remembers who you are.
Your deeds are unattributed.
Your name carries no recognition.
A creature may attempt to reclaim its memory of you by succeeding on a DC 30 History, Religion, Investigation check, or other relevant check, as appropriate (decided by the DM). On a success, memories return gradually over 3 days:
Day 1: Only negative memories return.
Day 2: Neutral memories return.
Day 3: Positive memories return.
On a failure, the creature cannot attempt the check again for 7 days.
For the next 30 days:
Whenever you interact with a creature that does not remember you, it must succeed on a DC 21 Wisdom or Intelligence saving throw or forget your most recent interaction at the end of the encounter. (Curable — each successful save reduces the remaining duration by 10 days.)
You constantly appear as if under the effects of the blur spell to creatures that do not remember you.

name: The World
upright:
You immediately gain the benefits of a long rest. All conditions, curses, diseases, madness, and (Curable) effects affecting you end.
You are returned to your prime age.
Your ability scores each increase by 1 (to a maximum of 24).
You may choose one saving throw to gain proficiency in, and one saving throw you are proficient in to gain expertise.
You gain half proficiency in all skills you are not proficient in. You may choose one skill to gain proficiency in, and one skill you are proficient in to gain expertise.
For the next 30 days, you are treated as a fixed point in reality:
You cannot be forcibly teleported, banished, or plane shifted.
You are immune to effects that would alter your form against your will.
reversed:
Time stops for all creatures except you for 7 days. During this time:
You cannot meaningfully advance any long-term goal.
Whenever you attempt to complete a significant task, you must succeed on a DC 25 Wisdom saving throw.
On a failure, your effort stalls at a level of completion depending on the roll.
You cannot cause permanent changes to creatures or structures while time is frozen. Any such changes revert when time resumes.
When the 7 days end, you are frozen in time for 7 days as reality "catches up." You are aware but cannot act.
The most formidable foe previously slain by the drawer is returned to life in their prime.
Over the next 30 days, the Material Plane (or current plane) slowly phases with another random plane:
Geography subtly shifts.
Creatures from both planes begin crossing over.
Environmental traits bleed through.
At the end of the 30 days, the planes fully overlap for 24 hours before separating again.
